req.title: Account-level transaction limits (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-067 defines three testable zones: (1) a valid in-range limit update that enters a 2-hour cool-down pending state (positive/EP), (2) a limit submission that exceeds the regulatory cap and must return 422 (negative/NEG), and (3) a limit set exactly at the regulatory cap boundary which must be accepted (boundary/BVA). An additional state-transition case verifies the pending→active transition after the cool-down elapses (STC). A decision-table case covers the combination of multiple channels in a single payload where one channel is over-cap while others are valid, ensuring partial-invalid rejection (DT). Together these five tests satisfy ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4 coverage for equivalence classes, boundary values, state transitions, and error paths called out in the requirement.

tests.name: test_set_valid_daily_limit_returns_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token in <auth_fixture>)
Account <account_id> exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested limit (5000) is within the regulatory cap for the channel
expected: Response body contains effective_at timestamp approximately 2 hours in the future
Subsequent GET /accounts/{id}/limits shows the new limit as pending, not yet active
trace: R-FIN-067 §valid in-range limit update enters pending state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_set_valid_daily_limit_returns_pending(api_client, auth_headers, account_id):
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 5000}
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    effective_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_at"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    delta_seconds = (effective_at - now).total_seconds()
    assert 7100 <= delta_seconds <= 7300, f"Cool-down window unexpected: {delta_seconds}s"
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    limits = get_resp.json()
    online = next(l for l in limits["channels"] if l["channel"] == "online")
    assert online["pending_limit"] == 5000
    assert online["active_limit"] != 5000

tests.name: test_set_limit_exceeding_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token in <auth_fixture>)
Account <account_id> exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Regulatory cap for channel 'online' is known via <regulatory_cap_fixture> (e.g. 10000)
actions: Record response status and error body
expected: No pending limit record created for the account
trace: R-FIN-067 §error pathways: 422 when over regulatory cap
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_set_limit_exceeding_regulatory_cap_returns_422(
    api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap
):
    over_cap = regulatory_cap("online") + 1
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": over_cap}
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "LIMIT_EXCEEDS_REGULATORY_CAP"
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    limits = get_resp.json()
    online = next(l for l in limits["channels"] if l["channel"] == "online")
    assert online.get("pending_limit") is None, "No pending limit should be stored on rejection"

tests.name: test_set_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token in <auth_fixture>)
Account <account_id> exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Regulatory cap for channel 'online' is known via <regulatory_cap_fixture>
expected: HTTP 202 Accepted (limit at cap is valid, not over)
Pending limit equals the exact regulatory cap value
trace: R-FIN-067 §BVA: limit == regulatory_cap is the inclusive upper boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_set_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted(
    api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap
):
    cap = regulatory_cap("online")
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": cap}
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body["daily_limit"] == cap

tests.name: test_pending_limit_becomes_active_after_cooldown
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token in <auth_fixture>)
Account <account_id> exists with an active limit already set
A pending limit was submitted and <time_travel_fixture> can advance server clock past the cool-down
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with a valid in-range limit to establish pending state
Advance server time by 2 hours + 1 second via <time_travel_fixture>
expected: Before time advance: active_limit reflects old value, pending_limit reflects new value
After time advance: active_limit equals the previously pending value
pending_limit field is null or absent after activation
trace: R-FIN-067 §changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_pending_limit_becomes_active_after_cooldown(
    api_client, auth_headers, account_id, time_travel
):
    new_limit = 3000
    patch_resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "online", "daily_limit": new_limit},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 202
    pre = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", headers=auth_headers).json()
    online_pre = next(l for l in pre["channels"] if l["channel"] == "online")
    assert online_pre["pending_limit"] == new_limit
    assert online_pre["active_limit"] != new_limit
    time_travel(seconds=7201)
    post = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", headers=auth_headers).json()
    online_post = next(l for l in post["channels"] if l["channel"] == "online")
    assert online_post["active_limit"] == new_limit
    assert online_post.get("pending_limit") is None

tests.name: test_mixed_channel_payload_rejected_when_one_channel_exceeds_cap
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token in <auth_fixture>)
Account <account_id> exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Regulatory cap known for channels 'online' and 'atm' via <regulatory_cap_fixture>
Payload includes 'online' within cap AND 'atm' over cap
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with body containing two channels: online at valid limit, atm at cap+1
Record response status and error body
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity for the whole request
Error references 'atm' channel as the violating field
No pending limit stored for either channel (atomic rejection)
trace: R-FIN-067 §error pathways: 422 when over regulatory cap
DT: valid x invalid channel combination must still fail atomically
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_mixed_channel_payload_rejected_when_one_channel_exceeds_cap(
    api_client, auth_headers, account_id, regulatory_cap
):
    valid_online = regulatory_cap("online") - 1
    over_atm = regulatory_cap("atm") + 1
    payload = {
        "limits": [
            {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": valid_online},
            {"channel": "atm", "daily_limit": over_atm},
        ]
    }
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    violations = [v["field"] for v in body["error"].get("violations", [])]
    assert "atm" in violations
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits", headers=auth_headers).json()
    for ch in get_resp["channels"]:
        assert ch.get("pending_limit") is None, f"Unexpected pending on {ch['channel']}"
